question.text: A short put at strike 83 with premium Rs. 0.075. At expiry USDINR is 82.50. What is the loss per unit?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Short put loss = Intrinsic paid out - Premium received = (83 - 82.50) - 0.075 = 0.50 - 0.075 = Rs. 0.425. **The Trap:** The seller keeps the premium but must pay the intrinsic value to the buyer. **Cross-Exam Context:** Short put P&L is tested in Series I and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Short put max profit = 0.075 (when USDINR ≥ 83). Loss begins below BEP (82.925) and increases as price falls.
